description_long: This object is a rectangular stone tomb, commonly known as a chest tomb or box tomb, typically found in graveyards. The tomb is constructed from weathered stone, with a flat slab lid and vertical sides adorned with simple geometric engravings, including diamond and lozenge shapes. The surface shows signs of aging, with discoloration, moss, and erosion visible along the lower edges and corners. The corners are slightly fluted, adding a subtle decorative element to the otherwise austere design. The top surface appears to have faint inscriptions, though they are not fully legible from the images. The tomb sits directly on the ground and is solidly built, suggesting it is intended as a permanent grave marker.
description: A weathered rectangular stone chest tomb with geometric engravings and a flat slab lid, showing signs of age and moss along the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main face with a large lozenge-shaped engraving and visible weathering along the bottom edge.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a diamond-shaped engraving and fluted corner details, with less visible weathering.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with a lozenge-shaped engraving but more pronounced discoloration and surface cracks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a diamond-shaped engraving and relatively cleaner stone surface.
